And that's what we'll be talking about on episode number one. Hey everyone, welcome to Funnel Hacker TV. Now I'm excited because this is an idea we've had for a long time and just excited to finally have a chance to bring it to you guys. Now a couple of things. First off, if you want one of these funnel hacker t-shirts for free, if you check them out here, they're amazing. That's what the front looks like here. So that's kind of a gift we have for you guys. Thanks for checking out ClickFunnels and building your very first funnel. All right, now before I kind of show you behind the scenes of my funnel and over future episodes, everybody else's funnels, we decided that we wanted to create kind of a hacker code of ethics that I think all of us need to follow because first off, if you don't follow these things, it's just not cool. Second off, if you do follow these things, you're going to have a ton of value and help make this entire process and make your funnels and other people's 1,000 times better. So these are the funnel hacker code of ethics. Number one is don't steal. This is a big one because I see this all the time. It drives me crazy. We do not want you stealing images from other people's funnels. You're not still copy. You're not still anything from anybody else's funnel. The goal is to look at a funnel and to model it. That is what is hackers here with funnel hackers. We do not steal. We look and we model. That's number one. Number two is funnel hackers always give credit. If you borrowed ideas from somebody else's funnel and they ask you about it, just give credit. Give credit where credit is due. I'm a big believer in that and everyone here and part of our community is as well. And number three is always add value. Don't just take somebody else's funnel and model 100% and just have it there. Try to always think like what can I do to add value or make this better or have new ideas, new tests, new tweaks, and things you can do to make it better. And then when you find those things out, please report them back to the community because that's what we're about here. All of us funnel hackers love sharing and giving and if you do that, you'll help make the community even better. Okay, so now I'm going to do it now is I want to show you guys the dot com secrets book funnel. Now, if you haven't seen this funnel yet, I'll have links down below so you can kind of check it out. But the book funnel is basically was selling this, my new book. And this is a book I spent literally over 10 years of my life earning what is inside of this book. And then a year writing this book and putting it together and sharing a whole bunch of really cool strategies. Everything from how we pick a market, how we start growing that market, where we go, how we build lists, how we drive traffic, the sales funnels we use, the sales scripts, everything that we teach and we do is in here inside the book. You'll see like literally everything. You'll see all of our sales scripts. You'll see my funnels. You'll see sample pages like everything we do and we teach is all in here. Like literally everything. Check that. I mean, you guys are gonna love this book. So if you don't have it yet, get the book. But I want to, today's, the goal today is kind of show you guys behind the scenes of this book funnel. The first thing that I'm gonna sketch out here on the whiteboard so you can see conceptually what it is. Then we're gonna actually jump on our computer. I'll show you guys the pages. I'll break them down, show you step by step. And the end of that I'm gonna actually show you guys the way you can get this funnel for free. Okay, so that's kind of the game plan. Let me sketch out kind of what happened here inside this book funnel. So again, the offer, if you look at what our offer is, the core thing is a free, free book. Now if you've studied any of my stuff at all, you know I talk a lot about creating free plus shipping offer. Something free you can give out there to put your best product, your best service in somebody's hands. One of the first times we did this, we did this, not the first time but recently about 18 months ago we did this with another book. And it was amazing people got this book in their hands and initially we were gonna sell it for about $200. I thought, you know what, I'm just gonna give away for free and see what happens. We gave away for free, people got this book in their hands and it completely transformed my business. They had a chance to see what we do and how we do it. It was just such an amazing experience. And so for me I'm a big fan of free plus shipping offers, getting one of your best products inside of customers hands, which is why we did that with the book funnel. Now obviously I'm not gonna make much money at all. I'm probably gonna go bankrupt or broke if I just go and just give away millions of books, right? So that's our strategy to monetize it. And so I'm gonna walk you through kind of our strategy. So here's the book. The first page when you go on the funnel, as you can see, this page I'm really proud of, this is a page, this entire funnel is built out in ClickFunnels. But if you look at it, there's kind of a two step opt-in form. Come here, you watch and there's a video, which is an amazing video. It's got kind of the visuals from where we filmed this part here that made the book cover. And then right hand side over here, step number one asks you for what is your shipping address? So someone puts in their shipping. And then down here I have a bunch of copy to kind of help sell the book. Even though it's a free product, you still gotta sell it. And then when someone comes in here, they put in their shipping address and at the end of it they click submit and take some step number two, which then opens up. When you go to step number two, this kind of erases and then it has the next page here that asks for your credit card. So that's kind of what this page looks like. They put their credit card information and then between the point of the credit card information and you have your order button right here, we have a little box that we call an OTO bump, an order form bump, which basically is, there's like two lines of copy. You'll see it says, hey, you're buying the book right now. How would you like the audiobook plus a bonus chapter for an extra $37, right? And it's a little bump offer there. And then afterwards there's a button for them to click submit to actually order the product. So that's the first page in this sequence. Like I said, I'm going to show you here in a second the actual pages, but conceptually understand that's step number one. Now after somebody, after they go through that process, then we take them over to our first upsell. On this page, there's a video of me right here talking and then we sell, I believe, over here talks about the product and it's $197. And then over here, we've got our add to cart button. That's step number two in the sequence. Step number three now, we have another video. In this product, we sell for $297. There's an order button here. And then over here is our thank you page. And it's a couple of cool things. So before I kind of show you the cool things here, I'm going to walk you through everything here. If you look at the pricing here on the first offer, the book is free. It's free, but we charge $7.95 shipping anywhere in the United States. And then the order form bump, so the OTO bump is $37. And again, the book, as you know, is a free book. The bump is the audiobook version of the book. Then our first upsell is it's $197. This is our instant traffic systems. So it's our traffic product. These people drive traffic. This is our webinar perfect webinar system, which is $297. And so this is kind of the sequence. Now one thing we found out, and I'll talk more about this in the Camtasia video, is that a big reason why this process works and why it worked is because of offer sequencing. Making sure you pick the right offers to go step one and step two and step three. Things have to make logical order. When we were doing a book funnel similar to this in the past, one of the big mistakes we made is that, for example, the book I was selling was a book on conversion. And so I thought, OK, people buying this, they're interested in conversion. So my first upsell was more stuff about conversion. And nobody was buying. I was so confused. What I realized is that when someone comes into a sales funnel and they buy the first product, they're coming to buy it because they have some kind of pain, right? They have an itch that's bothering them. And when they buy that, even though they haven't got the book yet or got the product yet, in their mind that itch has been scratched and it's gone. And so if I offer them more of that same thing on step number two, nobody buys it because that itch has been scratched. So you have to figure out after they buy the first thing, that problem has been solved in their mind. What's the next logical thing? What's the next issue they're going to run into? And that's your next upsell needs to be. So for us, we made it where the first is dot com secrets, the book, which is teaching about marketing and things like that. The first upsell now is traffic. Like you buy this thing, teaching you how to structure your business. Now what do you need? Why do you need traffic? You know, having a sales funnel is good. If I don't have traffic, what am I going to do? So the first upsell is all about traffic. Now you got traffic. Now that itch has been scratched. I figure I get traffic into their sales funnel. What's next they need? You got to know how to sell on these sales funnels. Now we have our sales product right here. And so that's why the offer converges really well is because the sequencing was set up the right way. Now on the thank you page, then right here, when they buy that, we basically have the product that they got, takes the members there where they can get access to everything they bought. But then we created something that we call an offer wall. And the offer wall basically has just different links to other products that we have. So there's, I think, three of our other free plus shipping products I put on this offer wall page here. There's a link to one of our training webinars. And there's just a couple of different offers here. It's kind of like, hey, thanks so much for buying. By the way, here's all the rest of our stuff. Here's everything else we sell. If you wanted these things, go check them out. And that's kind of how the process and the flow works. Now a couple other real cool things in this funnel that click funnels has given us and makes this magic is when somebody comes right here and they fill out step one on the credit card, not everybody who puts in step one fills out step two, which is putting in your credit card information. They put in their shipping address and they don't put in the credit card for whatever reason. And so what's cool is that click funds allows us to do a really cool email sequence just to those who don't finish step number two. Somebody puts in info in step number one, they click submit, and then they wait. And if they haven't bought in three hours, three hours later, click funds and then send them down to email and says, hey, thanks so much for filling it up. But so you didn't finish your order, why not? And it pushes them back. And then a day later sends out another email. And day later sends out another email. Keep sending out these emails, getting them to fill out their credit card if they don't do it. And as soon as they fill out their credit card, it says, okay, they fill out their credit card. And it takes them off that list and moves them on to another list, which then is them promoting the upsells and the other pieces in there. And so that's one of the cool, really powerful things I'll show you that makes this book funnel magic, the click funnels does. And so that right there, you guys, that's kind of the overall sequence. I want you to understand that whenever I go in a funnel, I like to buy it. I go through everything I said, okay, what did it look like? Like what did everything look like? What was their process? What were the pages involved? What were the price points? What was the actual offers? And I can go, I go into even more detail, for example, when I'm funnel hacking somebody, when I buy this product, I'll make a whole list and say, okay, when I bought this product right here, what was the offer? Well, the offer was, it was an ebook with 37 pages. It was 16 videos. It was, and I like exactly what their offer is. It doesn't mean I'm going to copy their offer, but I like to know what it is. Okay. If someone buys this right here, what does the offer actually look like? Okay. If they buy this, what's the offer look like? Because I want to be able to see that because again, I'm not going to copy their stuff. I need to understand what those things are. So when I'm making my episode, if I want to charge $197 for an episode, what did Russell put in? He had this, these are all the elements he had in his. You know, I need something similar to provide the same amount of value as he was providing in this step. And so that's why for me, I funnel hack each of the pieces, right? The process is big. The email sequences are big. The offer, what each offer is, what the hook is on the offer, how they sell them, what the videos say. I go as far typically as actually getting each of these videos. I usually get those and I transcribe them all so I can see exactly what is being said and how they sell each of these things. And again, not because I'm copying, okay? It's against the code of ethics for all funnel hackers. We don't copy, but we model, right? I want to see what their video is doing and how they're pitching and what their offers. If I understand that, then I can create something that's similar. And that's kind of how this whole funnel hacking process works. Okay. So again, guys, that's kind of, this is, that's the overall view of what the funnel looks like from a, from a sketched out standpoint. Okay, so here is the actual pages inside of the dot com secrets free book funnel. And I'm excited to show you guys this. I was just looking at it and basically this funnel is not super complicated. There are five different pages, actually kind of like four and a half different pages as you'll see here. And already in the first seven days since we launched this, this funnel has done over $328,000 and it's doing very, very well. Obviously we are driving a lot of launch traffic here, but also it was it was created to be able to drive long term consistent traffic from cold media. And so as you'll see some of the different aspects that I go through here, it'll make a lot of sense. So you come here, we got a really cool video that kind of promotes it. But the main thing I want to show you here is it's a two step order form. And we kind of model this initially after the CPA networks. If you look in there, they have a lot of these free plus shipping trial offers and selling supplements and things like that. And for the most part, you know, the ethics of a lot of CPA marketers aren't always the top level. I'm not a huge fan of how CPA works as a whole. But conceptually these guys test things like crazy. And one thing you'll notice is a lot of them, they do these two step order forms. So step number one is where should we ship your supplement or where should we ship this thing? And step number two now is give me your credit card. And so we started testing these about two years ago and saw just amazing results. In fact, it's funny, you know, a typical squeeze page that does well might convert it, you know, 30, 40, 50% or more. And right now the people land on this page that fill out step number one here, all these fields, name, email, phone number, shipping address, all the different pieces. We're at about 60% opt-in rate, which is just amazing. And I wouldn't believe that until I tested it. You know, some of my CPA friends had told me that in the past, I didn't believe it. And after we started running it, we saw that it was true. And so I don't know if those conversions will last long term, especially as we start turning on more cold traffic. But right now for it with warm traffic is pretty consistently at 60% fill in step number one. And part of the reasons is because it's so non, you know, like the you're not asking for a credit card, even though, you know, we tell people like step to produce your billing, we say there's credit card. Like we give them tons of information, but people just regardless, they fill out really, really quick and easy. Hey, where's your ship your book? Hey, ship it right here. And then step two is I'll show you here in a minute. Ask for the credit card. So this is kind of step number one. Obviously a headline here, a video, and then just some more stuff down here talking about what the book is. I had a countdown clock. This is when our free offer will disappear. And then down here, these are some of the other funnels that will happen in the launch sequence. And I'll kind of talk about some of these here in a little bit. He said, one thing I do different than most people's actually sell the product. I'm a big believer in that. Like even cause, even though it's free, you still got to sell it. I give away, I think it's like 10 hours worth of training from our live workshops they get and how much they talk about. It's not a catch. And I just kind of just go over everything. So I usually have all this stuff happening in the top of the fold. And then down below, I kind of open up and have a bunch of other things. So that's step number one. Like I said, we get about 60% of the people fill that land on this page. Fill out step one, which is awesome. And then from here, if they fill that, click on submit button, takes you now to the second page, which is just kind of refresh this page right here. And now it asks for their credit card number and has the shipping cost for the book here. And then there's this one little button down here. This is what we call an order form bump. And we start testing these about five or six years ago. And we first started doing it. We were, we were, it was a for continuity program. And what the bump was was, hey, instead of paying $37 a month, how would you like to pay $97 a month? And we got like 60% of people would start clicking this button. It was amazing. Now for me, I don't typically like doing continuity offers on a friend and offer like this. I like doing just straight, like here's what it is. And so we don't have any continuity, you know, you know, bumps on, on a page, but this is one where it's just a bump where they get the audio book version of this book, as well as this as a secret chapter for just an additional $37. They click on that, boom, and it adds to the order. And, uh, and that's it. And they click this. Now in the past, our reform bumps, we've had, you know, up to the higher end, 60% a lot of them are 40%. I think our average, um, across all the different pages, we have these order form bumps is like 30, I think 37 or 38% take that offer. Uh, with this launch following, I think we're at 20, 23, 24% are taking this right now. So, uh, it's, it's a huge increase in our front end revenue. It's amazing. Um, and you recommend doing order for months on anything you guys are doing, especially if you have something that you can explain in like a sentence or two, like if it's really complicated, it's not a good for an order form bump. But if you say, Hey, here's one real quick thing. Uh, it just works like magic. So that is kind of what the order form bump is. And all that's happening is this little, this little field right here is changing out. It's super easy to do inside of click funnels. Now, after they do that, click on ship my book. And then we take them now to, to upsell number one, uh, which they get in this page is built in click funnels. It's just one of our little elements and click funnels. It says, wait, your order is not yet complete. Don't close this page. Watch this video. And there's a video of me here saying, first off saying, thank you so much for ordering the book. You're going to love it. And I say, I got a really special offer for you guys. After you have the book next scene, you use traffic and I have the video kind of selling, uh, our instant traffic hacks product. Um, and then right here we've got this across here. I have a special offer, uh, the 80% off the retail price of what we normally sell it at. I found that special offers, like because it's special offer, you got to get people a good price. And so we do get people 80% discounted by here, which helps conversion. Uh, we allow this page out of 15 minute, um, clock that counts down to zero. So I've been on this page for a little while. As you can see, it's already down to zero, but I don't, I don't say this offer disappears and hit zero. Cause it's not true. I just have, I just have a countdown clock just counting for 15 minutes. Um, and it causes urgency, and I'm not like really saying here's urgency or scarcity. I just, I like having countdown clocks cause I like having motion on a page and it, um, it makes people uneasy. Like when I say countdown clock, I have this uneasiness. Even doesn't say anything. Just it's counting. And so I like having it there. So I have that countdown clock over here. I kind of a recap with the offers have what the prive, what the price was before the discount and what they're going to be paying here. Uh, picture the card. I kind of like, and then, uh, down here we have the, the no thanks link. Now a couple of cool things that we learned that we, as we were testing this process, one of them is right here. I've yes, upgrade my order. And we had that before people click on there and it'll get billed 197. And we were getting, um, a lot of people who got billed, who didn't realize it or whatever. And so, um, some of those bills didn't go through because people would call and complain. So we add this little subtext here, which is easy to do inside of click funnels. It says clicking on this button will charge your card $197. Now we did that. Our, our, um, complaints went to almost zero, which is awesome. I had just a little screenshot of what's inside the product. And then for my no thanks link, I also like a negative qualifier. I used to always say no, thanks. Take me to the members area now, which is a benefit to somebody. Right. You don't really want your no thanks link as a benefit. So I changed it to no, thank you. Please don't add instant traffic hacks to my members area at this huge discount. Okay. And then click on there. It takes them to, when they click on here, it takes them to, um, OTO number two, which again, you know, it says similar style. I changed the coloring. So I want people to think it's the exact same offer. Sometimes when it looks too similar page to page, I think it's the same offer and people get upset and they, they freak out. So I changed the color of this. I had the, the, uh, the, um, the progress bar moving further down. Had a red, red image here. There's a video me now explaining, um, our next offer, which is our sales product, our perfect webinar short, uh, secrets product. Um, and then I kind of get against similar model discount countdown clock. Um, the information about it, the price, um, the, uh, the, the text underneath it, the card, and then the negative qualifier notes. It's very similar to last page. Uh, they click on there. No, thanks. And then we take them to the, uh, the order confirmation page. Uh, excuse me. Okay. Now here's the order confirmation page where basically it says, thanks so much for ordering your book. A lot of times you guys think phones have to be too complicated. They're not. Again, the process is, uh, step one here, um, step two is this. And again, one cool thing, uh, and there's an email sequence. So they, they fill out step one and they don't fill out step number two. Then we actually have a full email sequence, um, that goes out to them to get them to, to, uh, to fill out step, uh, step number two. Um, I have email goes out immediately. It said, excuse me, it goes out three hours later. There's an email goes out one day later. And it goes out two days later, um, to kind of make, get them come back and felt their credit card. If they didn't, if they only did step one here and not step two number here. And then the next page is OTO number one, then OTO number two, and then our offer wall. And there you guys is anatomy of a million dollar free plus shipping book funnel. Okay. To end out episode number one of funnel hacker TV, um, I want to share with you guys some closing thoughts and I'm going to write these out in green because green equals money. All right. So I've got three closing thoughts for you guys. The first thing is when you're creating any kind of business and you want to attract new customers and get them into your business and get them to love you and to trust you and want to keep on giving you money. First step is you've got to do something, something very, very cool. So, uh, what I recommend is, as you can see from this funnel right here and from this book and from a lot of the market, we do is, um, the first thing that I recommend doing, if you want to start growing good companies, figure out what's your best product, what's the coolest thing you have, something that's worth talking about. You could share with the entire world and give it away. Don't even charge for it. Charge free plus shipping, you know, just enough for them to prove that they're serious, send it out to them. Cause if you do that, um, you're going to create faithful audience, people who love you who are going to keep on giving you money because you're sharing your best stuff with them. So that's step number one, um, is that number two is that what you're going to create. When you create a funnel like this, your goal short term is not to make money. I know that sounds so weird to people, but my goal when I create a funnel like this is not to make a ton of money. My goal from this is if I can break even, then this, this is a winner. Cause if I can, if I can add new customers to my customer, to my list, I can grow my business and I can do that at break even, then that's a win. Okay. So if I, if I go and let's say, um, I spend, you know, if I spend $40 in Facebook ads and PPC, whatever it is to sell one copy of this free book, but during this funnel on the, I averaged $40 and I break even, you may think, oh, it's a little, I lost, right? I spent $40 and made $40. I broke even, but the reality is you didn't, you made, you added a whole bunch of customers. People went through this process. They all fell down here and they became, uh, they became customers. And that's kind of the, the goal is to build customers initially. And so that's my, my, my biggest focus and same focus for you. Create a funnel like this without a goal of making anybody just to try and break even if you can do that, you've got a winner. And then the last thing is that the, the key metric I look for in this, in this model, most people don't even ever look at this. What's my average cart value for every single person who orders a book? How much on average do I make from them during this cart? Okay. We call this average cart value. Okay. That is the magic term. ACV. Okay. And the reason why that's so important for me is I know that for every book, I give away my average cart value. If it's $33, then I know that I can spend $33 to give away a book at break even. If my average cart value is $72, I know I can give, spend $72 to give away a book. Can you buy, say Russell, how do you figure out the math? How do you figure out where your average cart value is? It's very simple. Run your campaign for a little bit. And let's say you've sold 10 books or a hundred books or a thousand books. You take those thousand books, you take how much money you made from that entire process from this funnel, and then you divide that. You take the, the 10 books, let's say you made $10,000 and boom, you've got a hundred dollar average cart value. That's how you do the math. I'm not a mathematician, but that's a number worth figuring out. Cause as soon as I know that, I know how much money I can spend to acquire a customer. Uh, and that's kind of the game plan. You guys, so those are my closing thoughts. I hope that helps you out. If you're stuck on any of the terminology or things I'm talking about, get the book. We talk a lot about the stuff in the dot com secrets book. It's worth getting, uh, come to the funnel hacking live event. We're going to be breaking down tons of funnels and keep on watching funnel hacker dot TV. Thank you guys again so much for being here today. Hope you enjoyed episode number one and I'll see you guys again next week.
